title: Phyllanthus fasciculatus
intro: Phyllanthus fasciculatus, also known as Amla or Indian gooseberry, is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Phyllanthus fasciculatus include its ability to support liver health, boost the immune system, and act as an antioxidant, which helps to protect against cell damage and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, tannins, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Phyllanthus fasciculatus is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phyllanthus fasciculatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant is classified within the genus Phyllanthus, which is a group of plants commonly known as leaf flowers or Phyllanthus. Phyllanthus fasciculatus is further classified as a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Phyllanthus fasciculatus also includes its placement within the class Magnoliopsida, which includes most flowering plants. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
